text.title: Team Mode CLI Flag (`--team-config` Overlay) Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules grounding the team-mode-cli-flag methodology plus the `skip-this-methodology` safety rule. Each rule pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Faion CLI MUST accept both `--team-config <path>` and `FAION_TEAM_OVERLAY=<path>`. CLI flag takes precedence over env.
rule.rationale: Two surface areas: per-invocation flag for ad-hoc overrides, env for system-wide enforcement. CLI > env > personal is the standard 3-layer order.

rule.statement: Every field in the overlay MUST have a declared type and source (signing block). Free-form prose without a typed schema is rejected.
rule.rationale: Untyped overlays let `model: latest-greatest` slip in unchecked. Typed + signed values are falsifiable on load.

rule.statement: The overlay names one accountable owner (handle, email, or org role) in `signed_by`. No `team` or `we` as owner; no plural sign-off list as the source of truth.
rule.rationale: Plural ownership = no ownership; future readers need one named human to chase when the overlay misbehaves.

rule.statement: Overlay carries a semver `version` and `last_reviewed` date. Faion CLI emits a warning on read when `last_reviewed` is older than 90 days.
rule.rationale: Overlays decay; without a review cadence they become silent landmines.

rule.statement: Faion CLI MUST verify the ed25519 signature of the overlay using a team public key before applying it. Unsigned or invalid-signature overlays MUST be rejected (not warned).
rule.rationale: An overlay that overrides the model or MCP allowlist is a high-privilege artifact; treating it as trusted by file path alone is how a compromised laptop ships team-wide config malware.

rule.statement: Skip this methodology when the preconditions in `## Applies If` are not all true, or any item in `## Skip If` holds. The decision tree routes to this rule on those branches.
rule.rationale: A methodology that runs on the wrong input wastes tokens and produces misleading artifacts. The explicit skip rule is the safety valve.
